reasons: Yve wants to control grouped enemies from range, and Tigreal can create the crowd-control opening that keeps enemies inside her zoning area.
Tigreal's frontline engage lets Yve follow from safer spacing instead of starting the fight herself.

proof.summary: Tigreal starts the fight with grouped crowd control, while Yve follows with area slow and damage control from behind the frontline.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages after Yve is close enough to cast.
Yve saves her main zoning skills for the real crowd-control window.
failureCases: Tigreal engages too far away from Yve.
The enemy spreads out before Tigreal commits.
Yve is flanked or interrupted before the follow-up lands.

reasons: Yve benefits when enemies are pulled into one area, because her zoning damage becomes easier to place.
Atlas can force a teamfight shape that gives Yve a clear follow-up area.

proof.summary: Atlas groups enemies with engage control, while Yve places area damage and slows over the controlled zone.
worksBestWhen: Atlas catches multiple enemies or a priority backline target.
Yve waits for Atlas's real engage before committing her main damage window.
failureCases: Enemies interrupt Yve before her zone pressure matters.

reasons: Carmilla can link clustered enemies, giving Yve stronger value from repeated area damage and slows.
Yve can punish linked enemies from safe range while Carmilla plays inside the fight.

proof.summary: Carmilla links enemies in a teamfight, while Yve layers area damage and slow pressure into the linked group.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla links multiple enemies before Yve commits.
Yve casts while enemies are still connected or forced to stay near the fight.
failureCases: Enemies separate before the link has value.
Yve is forced to reposition before dealing damage.

reasons: Minotaur gives Yve a frontline that can start fights or peel enemies away from her casting position.
Yve benefits from Minotaur's crowd control because it keeps enemies in her zoning area longer.

proof.summary: Minotaur protects the front of the fight and creates control windows, giving Yve safer time to cast from behind him.
worksBestWhen: Minotaur positions between Yve and enemy divers.
Yve casts after Minotaur starts the fight or peels the dive.
failureCases: Minotaur engages without Yve in range.
Enemy assassins reach Yve from the side.
Yve spends her key skills before Minotaur forces the fight.

reasons: Khufra can disrupt enemy movement so Yve has more time to apply area control.
Yve benefits when Khufra forces enemies to fight around choke points or objective entrances.

proof.summary: Khufra creates a disruptive frontline zone, while Yve adds slows and area damage over the same space.
worksBestWhen: Khufra engages near walls, choke points, or objective entrances.
Yve keeps safe spacing behind Khufra.
failureCases: Enemy flankers ignore Khufra and reach Yve.
The fight happens in open space where enemies can spread freely.
